projects.title: Brightrail — Angular 21 Component Library
projects.reportSummary: Publishable Angular 21 component library with Funfair playground and Midway consumer verification app.
projects.problem: Teams need consistent, accessible UI building blocks for Angular applications.
projects.solution: Built a standalone Angular 21 component library with Funfair playground and Midway consumer app for npm-style verification.
keyFeatures: Funfair dev playground on GitHub Pages
architectureHighlights: Library-first monorepo: brightrail + funfair + midway
Funfair uses source path mapping; Midway uses node_modules dist
Theme-friendly SCSS component APIs
Angular 21 peer dependency >=21.0.0 <22.0.0
useCases: Library author builds components and publishes to dist/
Developer installs brightrail via npm and imports standalone components
Reviewer browses live Funfair playground without local setup

sequences.title: Consumer integration flow
steps: Run npm run build:lib to produce dist/brightrail
Consumer app adds brightrail dependency
Import standalone components into feature modules
Verify styling and accessibility in consumer UI

scope: Reusable Angular 21 standalone UI component library
Funfair developer playground with source-mapped library builds
Midway consumer app for npm-style integration verification
GitHub Pages hosted component showcase
deliverables: Publishable brightrail library package in dist/brightrail
functionalRequirements: Library author can build, preview, and export standalone components
Consumer app can install and import components via npm-style dependency
Funfair playground documents each component with live examples
Midway app validates real-world consumer integration path
nonFunctionalRequirements: Angular 21 peer dependency: >=21.0.0 <22.0.0
Accessibility-oriented component patterns (WCAG-minded)
Theme-friendly SCSS token architecture
Node 24.x compatible build toolchain

risks.risk: Breaking changes across Angular major versions
risks.mitigation: Pin peer dependency range; maintain Midway verification app in CI
Expand Funfair examples per component with usage notes

projects.title: POS Mono — Full-Stack Platform (CicdPOS)
projects.reportSummary: Production-grade retail POS platform: Spring Boot API, Angular PWA, Tauri desktop, PostgreSQL, JWT RBAC.
projects.problem: Retail stores need one backend for catalog, inventory, checkout, shifts, refunds, and administration — with web and desktop clients.
projects.solution: Spring Boot 3 REST API with PostgreSQL, JWT role-based security, Angular Material web UI, and optional Tauri desktop shell.
keyFeatures: Full POS checkout with barcode and offline sync
architectureHighlights: API-first: pos-mono-api serves Angular UI and Tauri desktop
Layered REST with ApiResponse wrapper and ErrorCode
PostgreSQL persistence + Azure Blob for product images
Single-tenant store model with shared catalog and orders
useCases: Admin: manage users, settings, billing, backup, IP rules
Manager: catalog, inventory, reports, shifts, labels
Cashier: POS checkout, customers, orders, refunds, own shift

sequences.title: Checkout sequence
steps: Cashier POST /api/auth/login → JWT issued
Open shift if required by store policy
Scan/search products and build cart
POST /api/orders → persist order, items, inventory update
Print or email receipt to customer

scope: Spring Boot 3 REST API with PostgreSQL persistence
Angular Material PWA for staff workflows (cashier, manager, admin)
Optional Tauri desktop client for dedicated POS terminals
Catalog, inventory, checkout, shifts, refunds, and reporting
deliverables: pos-mono-api — REST API with JWT RBAC
pos-mono-ui — Angular PWA with offline POS support
Architecture, ER, sequence, and deployment diagrams
functionalRequirements: Staff authenticate via JWT with role-based access (ADMIN, MANAGER, CASHIER)
Cashiers run POS checkout with barcode scan, cart, and receipt generation
Managers manage catalog, inventory, shifts, labels, and reports
Admins manage users, company settings, billing, backup, and IP access rules
Offline orders queue locally and sync when connectivity is restored
Product images stored in Azure Blob Storage
nonFunctionalRequirements: PWA offline mode with IndexedDB and service worker sync
HCI UI standards: compact tables, 40px touch targets
Structured API responses via ApiResponse wrapper and ErrorCode enum
Access logging and IP allow/block lists for security audit
Stateless API tier for horizontal scaling

apiDomains.capabilities: CRUD, search, barcode, bulk upload, images

risks.mitigation: Idempotent sync endpoints and conflict resolution in service layer
Short-lived tokens; IP restrictions for admin roles
Document tenant boundary; plan schema extension if needed

projects.title: BrightBubbles — Kids Learning Adventure
projects.reportSummary: Kid-friendly bubble color-pop learning game: Angular + Phaser 3 + Capacitor. 20 levels, 3 age modes, offline-first, privacy-first (no login, no ads).
projects.problem: Children need engaging, age-appropriate learning games that work offline without accounts, ads, or data collection.
projects.solution: BrightBubbles combines an Angular app shell with Phaser 3 game scenes and Capacitor mobile packaging. Progress stays on-device only — see PRIVACY.md on GitHub.
keyFeatures: Landing, splash, age mode, world map, Balloon Pop, rewards, parent zone
20 levels × 3 age modes (Tiny Learner, Explorer, Smart Challenger)
Star rewards, stickers, local progress persistence
Offline-first PWA — no login, no ads, no data collection
Procedural Web Audio + Capacitor Android/iOS packaging
architectureHighlights: Angular shell (src/app/) for routing, pages, and services
Phaser 3 game engine (src/game/) for scenes, logic, and level data
Local-only progress storage — see PRIVACY.md on GitHub
Capacitor sync for native mobile builds
useCases: Child: landing → splash → age mode (Tiny Learner / Explorer / Smart Challenger) → world map → Balloon Pop → earn stars and stickers
Parent: access parent zone for oversight, settings, and help
System: persist stars, stickers, and level progress in local storage with procedural Web Audio

sequences.title: Play level flow
steps: Child selects a level on the world map (1 of 20 levels)
Angular route loads the Phaser 3 Balloon Pop scene
Gameplay loop scores pops based on age-mode difficulty rules
Progress service writes stars and completion to local storage
Return to world map with updated rewards and unlocked levels

sequences.title: Mobile build flow
steps: npm run build for production PWA bundle
npx cap sync to copy web assets to Android/iOS shells
npx cap open android or ios for native packaging

scope: Angular application shell with routing and services
Phaser 3 game engine integration for Balloon Pop levels
Capacitor packaging for Android and iOS
Offline-first local progress — no backend required
deliverables: BrightBubbles MVP (v1.0.0) — PWA and Capacitor mobile builds
Balloon Pop gameplay with age-scaled difficulty docs
Parent zone, help, and visual design guide in repo docs/
Use-case architecture diagram on portfolio site
functionalRequirements: Landing, splash, age mode selection, world map, Balloon Pop game, rewards, parent zone, and help screens
Child selects age mode and navigates 20 levels across Tiny Learner, Explorer, and Smart Challenger modes
Balloon Pop awards stars and stickers; progress persists locally between sessions
Parent zone provides oversight without requiring child accounts or server login
nonFunctionalRequirements: Offline-first PWA — gameplay works without network connectivity
Privacy-first: no login, no ads, no child data transmitted (local storage only)
Procedural Web Audio for lightweight sound effects without asset bloat
Capacitor packaging for Android and iOS native distribution
Unit tests via npm test; MVP version 1.0.0-mvp

risks.mitigation: Optional export/import progress feature in future release
Pin dependency versions; test on target mobile platforms

projects.title: Micro-URL — URL Shortener Service
projects.reportSummary: Spring Boot microservice for URL shortening with MySQL persistence and redirect analytics.
projects.problem: Long URLs are hard to share; teams need short links with redirect resolution.
projects.solution: Spring Boot microservice with MySQL, REST API for shortening, and HTTP redirect for resolution with optional click tracking.
keyFeatures: Short URL generation via REST API
HTTP 302 redirect on short code lookup
architectureHighlights: Stateless API tier for horizontal scaling
RedirectController separate from UrlMappingController
MySQL persistence for mappings and events
useCases: API consumer submits long URL and receives short link
Browser visitor opens short link and gets redirected
System logs click events for analytics

sequences.title: Redirect flow
steps: POST shorten URL → store UrlMapping in MySQL
Return short link to client
GET /{shortCode} → lookup mapping
Log ClickEvent → HTTP 302 to original URL

scope: URL shortening REST API with MySQL persistence
HTTP redirect resolution for short codes
Optional click event analytics per redirect
deliverables: Spring Boot microservice with Gradle build
functionalRequirements: API consumer submits long URL and receives shortened link
Browser visitor opens short code and is redirected to original URL
System logs ClickEvent records for analytics
Optional user authentication for API access
nonFunctionalRequirements: Stateless API tier for horizontal scaling behind load balancer
Docker/Kubernetes compatible deployment
CORS configured for SPA client integration

risks.risk: Short code collision under high volume
risks.mitigation: Use sufficient entropy in code generation; unique DB constraint
Add URL validation and optional blocklist in future iteration

projects.title: E-Commerce API & UI
projects.reportSummary: Classic three-tier e-commerce demo with Spring Boot API and Angular storefront.
projects.problem: Demonstrate end-to-end online store flows with separated frontend and backend.
projects.solution: Spring Boot REST API with JPA entities paired with an Angular storefront for catalog and checkout.
architectureHighlights: CheckoutController orchestrates order creation
Relational model: Product, Order, Customer, Address
Angular UI consumes REST endpoints
useCases: Shopper browses catalog and adds items to cart
System persists Order and OrderItem records

sequences.title: Checkout flow
steps: UI loads products from REST API
User builds cart client-side
POST checkout payload to CheckoutController
API creates Order + OrderItem rows
UI displays order confirmation

scope: Spring Boot REST API with JPA relational model
Angular storefront for catalog browse and checkout
Separated frontend/backend three-tier architecture
functionalRequirements: Shopper browses product catalog organized by category
Shopper builds cart and completes checkout via API
System persists Customer, Order, OrderItem, and Address records
CheckoutController orchestrates order creation transaction
nonFunctionalRequirements: Clean API/UI separation for independent deployment
Relational integrity across Product, Order, and Customer entities
REST endpoints consumable by Angular SPA

risks.risk: Cart state lost on page refresh
risks.mitigation: Persist cart in session or local storage in UI layer

risks.risk: No payment gateway integration in demo
risks.mitigation: Document as reference implementation; extend for production payments

projects.title: Kubernetes & Istio Automation
projects.reportSummary: DevOps script collection for Kubernetes cluster and Istio service mesh operations.
projects.problem: Platform teams need repeatable scripts for Kubernetes clusters and Istio service mesh operations.
projects.solution: Curated shell and YAML automation for cluster setup, mesh configuration, and operational DevOps workflows.
architectureHighlights: Scripts target kubectl and Istio control plane
Supports microservice workload traffic management
Reference material for platform engineering study
useCases: Platform engineer applies mesh policies and observability helpers
Developer references scripts for staging cluster setup
Self-study of K8s and Istio operational patterns

sequences.title: Typical ops workflow
steps: Clone repo and select target script
Run against kubectl cluster context
Istio sidecars apply traffic and security rules
Validate mesh health and workload routing

scope: Shell and YAML automation for Kubernetes cluster operations
Istio service mesh configuration and validation scripts
Reference material for platform engineering workflows
deliverables: Curated script collection in kubernetes-istio-scripts repository
functionalRequirements: Platform engineer can apply mesh policies using documented scripts
Developer can reference staging cluster setup procedures
Operator can validate mesh health after script execution
nonFunctionalRequirements: Scripts must declare compatible kubectl and Istio versions
Non-production cluster usage recommended for learning scripts
GitOps-friendly, reviewable script patterns

risks.mitigation: Document compatible versions per script folder

risks.risk: Script run against wrong cluster context
risks.mitigation: Require explicit context validation before destructive operations
